title: Getting Started
content:
# Getting Started

Welcome to the Developer Experience Platform (DXP). This guide will help you get up and running quickly.

## Prerequisites

- Node.js 20+
- pnpm 9+
- Git

## Installation

```bash
git clone https://github.com/your-org/dxp.git
cd dxp
pnpm install
```

## Running Locally

```bash
pnpm dev
```

This starts all MFEs and the shell in parallel. The shell is available at `http://localhost:3000`.

## Next Steps

Explore the [Architecture](architecture) guide to understand how MFEs are composed at runtime.

children.title: Installation
children.content:
# Installation

Detailed installation instructions for each supported platform.

## macOS

```bash
brew install node pnpm
```

## Linux

```bash
curl -fsSL https://get.pnpm.io/install.sh | sh -
```

## Windows

Use WSL2 with Ubuntu for the best experience.

## Verify Installation

```bash
node --version  # v20+
pnpm --version  # v9+
```

children.title: Quick Start
children.content:
# Quick Start

Get a working DXP instance in under 5 minutes.

## 1. Clone the repository

```bash
git clone https://github.com/your-org/dxp.git && cd dxp
```

## 2. Install dependencies

```bash
pnpm install
```

## 3. Start development servers

```bash
pnpm dev
```

## 4. Open your browser

Navigate to `http://localhost:3000`. Log in with `dev@dxp.local` / `password`.

> **Tip:** All data is mocked in development. No external services are required.

title: Architecture
content:
# Architecture

DXP uses a **Micro-Frontend (MFE) architecture** powered by Webpack 5 Module Federation.

## Shell + MFE Composition

The shell is a thin orchestrator with zero hardcoded MFE imports. All composition is config-driven via a runtime JSON registry.

```
shell (Webpack host)
  └── loads remoteEntry.js dynamically at runtime
        ├── mfe-feature-flags  (port 3001)
        ├── mfe-experiments    (port 3002)
        ├── mfe-logs           (port 3003)
        ├── mfe-api-explorer   (port 3004)
        └── mfe-docs           (port 3005)
```

## Key Principles

- **Zero shell coupling** — MFEs deploy independently
- **Shared singletons** — single React, single Router, single QueryClient per MFE
- **RBAC at the shell** — `ProtectedRoute` enforces `manifest.permissions`
- **Error isolation** — each MFE wrapped in `MFEErrorBoundary`

children.title: Module Federation
Registry System
children.content:
# Registry System

All MFE composition is driven by `apps/registry/registry.json` — fetched by the shell at boot.

## Schema

```json
{
  "mfes": [
    {
      "name": "featureFlags",
      "route": "/flags",
      "scope": "featureFlags",
      "module": "./App",
      "url": "https://cdn.app.com/featureFlags/v1/remoteEntry.js",
      "permissions": ["admin", "dev"],
      "enabled": true,
      "version": "1.2.0",
      "canaryPercent": 0
    }
  ]
}
```

## Deployment Flow

1. MFE team builds and uploads assets to CDN
2. CI writes new CDN URL into `registry.json`
3. Shell reads updated registry on next page load
4. **No shell redeploy required**

title: MFE Development

children.title: Testing Guide
Design System
children.content:
# Design System

All UI components must come from `@dxp/ui`. Never build custom components outside the design system.

## Available Components

| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `Button` | Primary action button with variants |
| `Badge` | Status indicator |
| `Input` | Form text input with error state |
| `Dialog` | Modal compound component |
| `Skeleton` | Loading placeholder |
| `ErrorFallback` | Standardised error display |

## Button Variants

```tsx
<Button variant="primary">Save</Button>
<Button variant="secondary">Cancel</Button>
<Button variant="destructive">Delete</Button>
<Button variant="ghost">View</Button>
```

## Badge Variants

```tsx
<Badge variant="success">Active</Badge>
<Badge variant="warning">Paused</Badge>
<Badge variant="destructive">Error</Badge>
<Badge variant="default">Draft</Badge>
```

## Tailwind

Extend `@dxp/ui`'s Tailwind preset — never define a standalone config:

```typescript
import { dxpPreset } from '@dxp/ui/tailwind';
export default { presets: [dxpPreset], content: ['./src/**/*.{ts,tsx}'] };
```

title: API Reference
content:
# API Reference

Internal API documentation for DXP platform services.

## Authentication

All API requests require a Bearer token:

```http
Authorization: Bearer <token>
```

Tokens are passed to MFEs via `MFEProps.auth.token` and must **never** be embedded in MFE bundles.

## Registry API

```
GET /registry.json
```

Returns the current MFE registry. Cached with `stale-while-revalidate`.

## Feature Flags API

```
GET    /api/flags          List all flags
POST   /api/flags          Create a flag
PATCH  /api/flags/:id      Update a flag
DELETE /api/flags/:id      Delete a flag
```

## Proxy API (API Explorer)

```
POST /api/proxy
```

Server-side proxy for API Explorer requests. Auth tokens are never exposed in the browser network tab.

children.title: Federation Contracts
children.content:
# Federation Contracts

`@dxp/federation-contracts` exports TypeScript types and Zod schemas for the MFE mount interface.

## Package

```
packages/federation-contracts/
  src/
    types.ts    # TypeScript interfaces
    schema.ts   # Zod schemas
    index.ts    # Barrel export
```

## Exports

```typescript
import type { MFEMountFn, MFEInstance, MFEProps } from '@dxp/federation-contracts';
import { MFEMountFnSchema } from '@dxp/federation-contracts';
```

## Zero React Dependency

This package has **no React dependency** — pure TypeScript + Zod. Any framework can implement the contract.
